headline: Meta AI cross-reference confirmation. Composite 29.4 → 28.6 (-0.8 sub-threshold; band Developing sustained). Cross-references the Meta Platforms parent in Fortune 500: NM v. Meta Phase 2 trial conduct (algorithm redesign mandate, platform-exit threat) materializes governance parent's Phase 2 conduct posture. The Meta AI division contributes the algorithm-design infrastructure that the NM injunction targets. SYS -0.1 reflects parent-company governance escalation (exit threat) propagating to AI Labs entry. INT -0.1 reflects same. Other dimensions unchanged.
key_evidence: May 4 2026 (today): NM v. Meta Phase 2 trial opened — sought injunctive relief includes mandatory algorithm redesign for minors. The Meta AI division is the technical infrastructure for the algorithms the injunction targets.
May 4 2026: Meta Platforms threatened to shut down FB/IG/WhatsApp in NM rather than comply — governance posture propagates from parent to AI Labs entity.
Cross-reference: meta-platforms-2026-05-04 proposal in fortune-500 index; this proposal reflects the AI Labs governance parent.
No standalone Meta AI evidence beyond parent-company governance signals in window.

evidence.finding: NM v. Meta Phase 2; algorithm redesign sought; Meta exit threat — governance parent conduct propagates.
Algorithm redesign mandate detail; addictive feature ban for minors.

scoring_rationale.SYS: 2.6 → 2.5 (-0.1). S1 Structural Justice and S5 Adaptive Capacity: parent-company exit threat as response to algorithm-redesign mandate signals AI Labs governance posture under regulatory pressure. The technical infrastructure for the targeted algorithms is the Meta AI division.
scoring_rationale.INT: 2 → 1.9 (-0.1). I1 Consistency Under Pressure: AI Labs stated mission of safety research is contradicted by parent-company governance posture of regulatory exit-threat over compliance.

math_check.validator_check: Drift well within 2.0pt per-cycle ceiling. Sub-threshold delta. Developing band sustained.

negativeSignals: Parent-company NM exit threat propagates to AI Labs governance posture; algorithm-redesign mandate targets Meta AI infrastructure.
positiveSignals: None in window beyond research-mission framing.
sector_context: Meta AI's score is partially derived from Meta Platforms parent-company governance. The NM Phase 2 trial creates the most direct AI Labs-relevant injunctive risk in the cluster — algorithm redesign requirements would directly affect Meta AI engineering practice.
open_actions_carried: Phase 2 ruling expected late May / early June — re-queue with parent
Watch: any actual algorithm change announcement
Watch: parent-AI-Labs decoupling — if Meta AI articulates separate posture
